title: Declare state, not commands
description: Use a module that describes the desired end state instead of a shell command that runs every time.
body: Use when a task should converge a host to a desired configuration. Do not reach for shell or command modules when a purpose-built module exists — raw commands run unconditionally and report changed every time, breaking idempotency. Actions: pick the module (package, copy, template, lineinfile, service) that declares the target state and lets Ansible decide whether a change is needed; inspect the module docs for its idempotency guarantees. Validation signals include a second run reporting zero changed for that task. Risk boundary: where a raw command is unavoidable, guard it with changed_when/creates so it does not falsely report change or run destructively twice.
